title: System and method for predictive compensation of uplink laser beam atmospheric jitter for high energy laser weapon systems
abstract: A system includes a target illumination laser (TIL) (210) configured to illuminate an airborne target (120, 240) with a TIL beam (235). The system also includes a beacon illuminator (BIL) (215) configured to transmit a spot of illumination (245) to an expected location on the target, where the spot of illumination is more focused than the TIL beam. The system further includes a camera (220) configured to receive an image of the spot reflected off the target. In addition, the system includes a controller (230) configured to determine an actual location of the spot on the target based on the received image. The controller is also configured to estimate a spot motion by correlating the actual location of the spot on the target with the expected location on the target. The controller is further configured to predict uplink jitter (316) of a high energy laser (HEL) beam generated by a HEL (205) based on the BIL spot motion, where the uplink jitter is caused by atmospheric optical turbulence (238).
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A system comprising: a target illumination laser (TIL) configured to illuminate an airborne target with a TIL beam; a beacon illuminator (BIL) configured to transmit a spot of illumination to an expected location on the target, wherein the spot of illumination is more focused than the TIL beam; a camera configured to receive an image of the spot reflected off the target; and a controller configured to: determine an actual location of the spot on the target based on the received image; estimate a spot motion by correlating the actual location of the spot on the target with the expected location on the target; and predict uplink jitter of a high energy laser (HEL) beam generated by a HEL based on the spot motion, the uplink jitter caused by atmospheric optical turbulence. 2. The system of Claim 1, wherein the controller is further configured to control movement of at least one fast steering mirror (FSM) to substantially cancel the predicted uplink jitter of the HEL beam. 3. The system of Claim 2, wherein the controller is configured to predict the uplink jitter by taking into account a round trip speed-of-light delay of the spot of illumination and an operational delay of a FSM servo associated with the at least one FSM. 4. The system of Claim 1 , wherein the controller is configured to predict the uplink jitter using an autoregressive input-output model. 5. The system of Claim 1, wherein the camera is a high-speed short wave infrared (SWIR) camera co-boresighted with the HEL. 6. The system of Claim 1, wherein: the TIL beam and the spot of illumination are at different wavelengths; and the camera is configured to receive both the image of the spot and an image of the target illuminated by the TIL beam. 7. The system of Claim 1, wherein the controller is configured to estimate the spot motion relative to one or more identified features on the target. 8. The system of Claim 7, wherein the one or more identified features on the target comprise a feature on a nose of the target. 9. The system of Claim 1, wherein the controller is configured to estimate the spot motion using an image processing algorithm. 10. The system of Claim 1, wherein the TIL beam illuminates the target in a short wave infrared (SWIR) band. 11. A method comprising: illuminating an airborne target with a target illumination laser (TIL) beam; transmitting a spot of illumination to an expected location on the target, wherein the spot of illumination is more focused than the TIL beam; receiving, at a camera, an image of the spot reflected off the target; determining an actual location of the spot on the target based on the received image; estimating a spot motion by correlating the actual location of the spot on the target with the expected location on the target; and predicting uplink jitter of a high energy laser (HEL) beam generated by a HEL based on the spot motion, the uplink jitter caused by atmospheric optical turbulence. 12. The method of Claim 11, further comprising: controlling movement of at least one fast steering mirror (FSM) to substantially cancel the predicted uplink jitter of the HEL beam. 13. The method of Claim 12, wherein the uplink jitter is predicted by taking into account a round trip speed-of-light delay of the spot of illumination and an operational delay of a FSM servo associated with the at least one FSM. 14. The method of Claim 11, wherein the uplink jitter is predicted using an autoregressive input-output model. 15. The method of Claim 11, wherein the camera is a high-speed short wave infrared (SWIR) camera co-boresighted with the HEL. 16. The method of Claim 11, wherein: the TIL beam and the spot of illumination are at different wavelengths; and the camera receives both the image of the spot and an image of the target illuminated by the TIL beam. 17. The method of Claim 11, wherein the spot motion is estimated relative to one or more identified features on the target. 18. The method of Claim 17, wherein the one or more identified features on the target comprise a feature on a nose of the target. 19. The method of Claim 11, wherein the spot motion is estimated using an image processing algorithm. 20. The method of Claim 11, wherein the TIL beam illuminates the target in a short wave infrared (SWIR) band.
